title: Contemplator/Final-Answer tag structure
description: Two-tag output format: <contemplator> for extended internal monologue (min 10,000 chars), <final_answer> for conclusions. Separates reasoning from output. Similar to Claude's native <thinking> blocks.

title: Exploration over conclusion principle
description: Never rush to conclusions. Keep exploring until solution emerges naturally from evidence. If uncertain, continue reasoning indefinitely. Question every assumption and inference.

title: Natural thought flow markers
description: Specific phrases that signal reasoning modes: 'Hmm... let me think about this...', 'Wait, that doesn't seem right...', 'Maybe I should approach this differently...', 'Going back to what I thought earlier...'

title: Embrace uncertainty and revision
description: Show work-in-progress thinking, acknowledge dead ends, frequently backtrack and revise. Don't force conclusions — say 'not possible' if reasoning leads there.

title: No evidence of effectiveness
description: No benchmarks, comparisons, A/B tests, or quantitative evidence that this prompt produces better outcomes than default behavior or native extended thinking.

title: No multi-turn or agent context
description: Prompt assumes single-turn Q&A. No treatment of how contemplative reasoning interacts with tool use, multi-agent workflows, or session continuity.

title: No structured phases or convergence
description: Reasoning is unstructured stream-of-consciousness. No phases, no verification loops, no agent dispatch. Relies entirely on the LLM's own judgment for when to stop exploring.
